v1.1.1.description:
## More Traps + UI Fixes

### Highlights
- Overhauled trap handling with a broader set of working traps, improved targeting, and legacy compatibility for older seeds.
- Reworked the AP shop flow so that recreated shops now respect the YAML-configured shop size, with better item previews and cleaner check labeling.
- Cleaned up AP shop behavior by removing leftover vanilla unlock requirements, so randomized entries no longer inherit old unlock or roguescore gates.
- Updated player-facing progress displays so shop and notification counters now show sequential progress like `1/50`, `2/50`, and so on instead of internal slot IDs.

### Changes
- Renamed the goal item to **NG+** and updated its tooltip to make it clear that it is the goal item.
- Reworked the AP shop/UI flow to better match how the recreated shop behaves in practice.
- Improved item preview handling in the shop UI.
- Cleaned up check labeling to make shop entries easier to read.
- Made trap shop entries cost **0 gold**. >:)
- Simplified the shop unlock pool by removing obsolete `base_cost` data.
- Replaced the old **Unlock Tadpole Power** and **Unlock Multipliers** AP items with cleaner hidden/progressive handling.
- Updated the default and sample YAML configs so all currently working traps are included by default.

### Fixes
- Removed unwanted vanilla shop requirement behavior from AP shop entries, preventing random items from inheriting broken legacy unlock conditions.
- Reworked trap application so status traps now affect the full party, companions/followers, and summons.
- Fixed the Confusion trap to use a correct BG3 status behavior.
- Removed broken or retired traps from generation **Sussur**, **Clown**, and **Overburdened** while preserving compatibility with older seeds that still contain them. Might add back later.
- Added a new set of working traps: **Bane**, **Blindness**, **Slow**, **Poisoned**, **Faerie Fire**, **Ensnared**, **Frightened**, **Burning**, **Hold Person**, **Silence**, and **Grease**.
- Added special handling for certain traps:
  - **Silence** now spawns on the party member with the strongest mental stat.
  - **Grease** now spawns on a random party member.

### Notes
- Legacy seeds with retired traps are still supported, but those traps will no longer appear in newly generated seeds.
v1.1.1.title: v1.1.1 - Trappy Trials

v1.1.2.description:
## Archipelago Parity Cleanup

### Highlights
- Aligned the repo with current Archipelago docs and main-repo expectations for world structure, manifests, docs, tests, and web metadata.
- Cleaned up and expanded AP world support files, including settings, docs, presets, groups, and generation tests.
- Fixed packaging and datapackage issues so the world now bundles correctly and uses canonical location names.
- Updated releases so `bg3tot.apworld` ships as a separate asset alongside the mod zip.

### Changes
- Added and cleaned up AP world support files and related metadata.
- Refreshed README, install docs, and build docs for the current setup and release flow.

### Fixes
- Fixed AP world packaging, including correct embedded unlock catalog bundling.
- Updated `archipelago.json` to match current Archipelago manifest rules.
- Fixed location naming/datapackage compatibility and avoided duplicate-ID alias issues.
- Revalidated the world against a fresh Archipelago main checkout with targeted core-facing tests.

### Notes
- `bg3tot.apworld` is no longer included inside the mod zip.
v1.1.2.title: v1.1.2 - Repo Refresh

v1.1.3.description:
## First Player Suggested Content Drop + Various Bug Fixes

### Highlights
- Reworked several filler/trap effects so they now apply party-wide, last one turn, and use cleaner condition-based implementations.
- Added support for `permanent_buff_target`, including AP options/presets and a default of `random_party_member`. - **Thank you chromaticwyvern**

### Changes
- Removed roguescore increase from the filler item pool for now. It may come back later in a different form, likely as a trap.
- Added a new cheese wheel trap that transforms the entire party into cheese wheels using `Target_WYR_PolymorhphCheese_Djinni`. - **Thank you extligh**
- Reworked trap behavior so all current traps apply to every party member for one turn.
- Replaced the silence trap with the silence condition applied directly to all party members.
- Replaced the grease trap with the prone condition.
- Added a user-file config option for Pixie Blessing. It is now off by default, but can be toggled on to force Pixie Blessing into the shop like vanilla. - **Thank you poofy_**

### Fixes
- Fixed quick checks showing in the wrong order in-game. They should now always display numerically.
- Local ItemSend notifications now use the check’s actual local earn sequence for tagging.
- Patched the shop-purchase rollback bug. Purchased AP shop checks are now mirrored into a pending replay file until the next successful save.
- On load, pending replay records are merged back into `state.shop_unlocks` before the shop is rebuilt, preventing lost purchases after a rollback.

### Notes
- Item ordering may still appear incorrect in the BG3-ToT client in some cases, even though the in-game ordering issue is fixed.
- Discontinued support for the in-game mod manager.
v1.1.3.title: v1.1.3 - Community Crafted

v1.1.4.description:
## Integrated AP Client Into Trials GUI + Reworked Repo/Build Path

### Changes
- Added an in-game version of the apworld client to the Trials of Tav GUI.
- Matched the existing client functionality in-game, so anything you could do through the external client is now available from the UI as well.
- Restructured the repo to include the full Trials of Tav Reloaded pak directly in the main project.

### Fixes
- Removed the old workflow of building over top of the Reloaded pak, which should make integration changes easier to track and maintain.

### Notes
- This is mostly a workflow and integration update, but it should make both development and in-game AP use a lot cleaner going forward.
v1.1.4.title: v1.1.4 - Menu Mania

v1.1.5.description:
## Configurable Multiworld Item Pool

### Highlights
- Unlock pool customization is much more flexible now.
- Multi-copy unlocks now get their own YAML/options sliders with catalog defaults.
- Configured non-filler unlocks are no longer lost when `shop_check_count` is too low.

### Changes
- Added individual sliders for multi-copy unlocks from `trials_unlock_catalog.json`, with a max of 100 copies each.
- `Include Equipment Fillers` is now the only toggle in that section. Off to disable single vanilla item checks.
- Updated sample YAML generation and docs to match.

### Fixes
- Shop checks now auto-expand as needed, so configured non-filler unlocks still make it into the multiworld.
v1.1.5.title: v1.1.5 - Not Enough Items

v1.1.6.description:
## DeathLink, Shop, & NG+ Changes

### Changes
- DeathLink now affects companions as well as core party members, so it applies more consistently across the whole active group.
- Added Progressive Shop Fragments. The Archipelago shop now unlocks in stages over time instead of becoming fully available all at once. **- xina_lily**
- Added NG+ shop gating along with a configurable NG+ cost slider. NG+ can now be tied to a chosen percentage of total Shop Fragments collected. **- zapony**
- Added new DeathLink Punishment settings, letting BG3 players choose how incoming DeathLinks are handled. **- reveriedivine**

### Fixes
- Receiving a DeathLink should now reliably force a reload. **- xina_lily**
- Expanded DeathLink punishment behavior to support wipes, random downs or kills, resource drains, or no punishment. **- reveriedivine**

### Notes
- NG+ progression can now be tuned more precisely for different world settings and pacing preferences.
- DeathLink behavior is now more flexible, both in who it affects and in how punishing it is.
- Thank you for your suggestions and bug finding.
v1.1.6.title: v1.1.6 - Fragment Framework

v1.1.7.description:
## 0.6.7, DeathLink, & Progression Balancing

### Changes
- Multiworld now forces a Progressive Shop Fragment into an early local check, making it much more likely that the BG3 player has at least one fragment available to spend early.
- Added two new DeathLink Punishment options: Remove Actions Random and Remove Actions All. These punishments strip action, bonus action, and movement only. **- zapony**
- Updated the existing Remove Resources Random punishment so it now selects targets randomly instead of always hitting the character with the highest mental stat. **- zapony**
- Adjusted XP filler item balance for better pacing.

### Fixes
- Cleared up the wording on the existing Remove Resources punishment to make it clear that it removes all resources, not just actions, and synced the title format to match the new punishments. **- zapony**
- Verified compatibility with Archipelago 0.6.7 for this patch.

### Notes
- Early shop access in Multiworld should feel more consistent now, especially in seeds where early fragment availability mattered. No more runs where the shop is never unlocked.
- Getting an early 3000 XP filler item felt too strong, so I removed it from the pool. 1000 XP drops are still enabled, and there will always be at least 10 by default.
v1.1.7.title: v1.1.7 - Progressive Patching

v1.2.0.description:
## Localization Update & Minor Bug Fix

### Changes
- Refactored all player-facing text to use the localization system, laying the groundwork for full i18n support across the mod.
- Added support for every default BG3 language:
  - English
  - French
  - German
  - Spanish
  - Latin American Spanish
  - Polish
  - Russian
  - Italian
  - Turkish
  - Brazilian Portuguese
  - Japanese
  - Korean
  - Simplified Chinese
  - Traditional Chinese
  - Ukrainian

### Fixes
- Fixed an issue where summons and companions being downed or killed could incorrectly trigger DeathLink. **- zapony**
v1.2.0.title: v1.2.0 - Multilingual Milestone
